title: poa secunda var. nevadensis
intro: Poa secunda var. nevadensis, also known as Nevada bluegrass, is a perennial grass native to the western United States and Canada, characterized by its blue-green foliage and narrow blades. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, attributed to its high saponin content. The health benefits of Nevada bluegrass include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and potentially supporting digestive health. Herbal preparations of Poa secunda var. nevadensis typically involve drying the leaves and stems, then using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
